title: Northstar x Redwood - Security review: Optimize quality gates + audit trail
preview: Northstar requested a security/controls deep dive focused on Redwood Optimize making cost-saving config changes (batching/caching/quantization) and how Redwood prevents silent quality regressions. Redwood walked through the “quality gate” enforcement model: proposed config diffs trigger static validation + eval-harn...
text: Northstar requested a security/controls deep dive focused on Redwood Optimize making cost-saving config changes (batching/caching/quantization) and how Redwood prevents silent quality regressions. Redwood walked through the “quality gate” enforcement model: proposed config diffs trigger static validation + eval-harness runs (smoke gate then full gate) against a baseline; only passing results can be applied. Overrides are time-bound, require an authorized approver, and emit audit log events. Rollout controller blocks canary promotion when gates fail and can auto-rollback if online quality signals degrade. Northstar asked about audit log contents, export to SIEM, RBAC, and whether customer prompts are stored; Redwood described artifact pointers, configurable retention, and enterprise log export options. Next steps: Redwood to share audit event schema/examples, SOC2 package, and a short ...

Redwood clarified hosted vs VPC/Private deployment options and what telemetry is captured by default. title: Acme Bank - Security review (KMS integration, rotation, audit evidence)
preview: Acme Bank security team reviewed Redwood Inference’s customer-managed KMS integration for Private deployments, focusing on envelope encryption, rotation workflow (including dual-decrypt window and rollback), and what audit evidence Redwood can provide. Acme emphasized quarterly rotation, segregation of duties, and c...
text: Acme Bank security team reviewed Redwood Inference’s customer-managed KMS integration for Private deployments, focusing on envelope encryption, rotation workflow (including dual-decrypt window and rollback), and what audit evidence Redwood can provide. Acme emphasized quarterly rotation, segregation of duties, and clear behavior during KMS degradation/outage. Redwood committed to sharing the Private KMS operator guide, rotation procedure/evidence checklist, and a sample audit-log export schema including rotation events, plus scheduling a follow-up deep dive on failure-mode behavior and operational runbooks.

title: AsterBank Security Review - Offline installs / signed bundles (Redwood Private)
preview: AsterBank reviewed Redwood’s planned/ongoing approach for signed offline bundles for Redwood Private installs in air-gapped environments. Key questions focused on (1) how Redwood proves integrity/provenance without network access, (2) signing key custody and rotation (including how customers update trust roots offli...
text: AsterBank reviewed Redwood’s planned/ongoing approach for signed offline bundles for Redwood Private installs in air-gapped environments. Key questions focused on (1) how Redwood proves integrity/provenance without network access, (2) signing key custody and rotation (including how customers update trust roots offline), (3) audit log evidence (local-only logging + SIEM export), (4) replay/downgrade protections, and (5) documented exceptions/break-glass. Redwood agreed to share a draft bundle verification guide, key rotation/runbook excerpts, and a sample audit event schema and support bundle output. AsterBank requested clarity on algorithm choices/FIPS posture and a written statement about fail-closed behavior when the key is unknown or signature invalid.

title: Cloud Marketplace Partner – Security collateral alignment (SOC 2, shared responsibility, listing requirements)
preview: AsterCloud Marketplace and Redwood aligned on the security collateral required for a marketplace listing and co-sell motion. Topics included: approved SOC 2 language and how to handle gated evidence requests (under NDA), ISO 27001 “alignment” wording (avoid certification claims), a shared responsibility model for Ho...
text: AsterCloud Marketplace and Redwood aligned on the security collateral required for a marketplace listing and co-sell motion. Topics included: approved SOC 2 language and how to handle gated evidence requests (under NDA), ISO 27001 “alignment” wording (avoid certification claims), a shared responsibility model for Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private deployments, data retention/residency language (no overpromises), and audit logging/SSO/RBAC statements suitable for the listing page. The group agreed on a review workflow: Redwood Security/GRC owns factual security claims; Product Marketing owns messaging; Partnerships owns final packaging for the marketplace submission. Action items include Redwood delivering a short “security & trust” blurb + shared responsibility table, and AsterCloud providing their listing questionnaire and required template sections.

title: Helios Defense - Air-gapped Redwood Private deployment deep dive (offline bundles + verification)
preview: Helios Defense walked through their air-gapped environment constraints (no outbound network, controlled inbound media, strict change control, limited time sync) and asked for an end-to-end, offline-verifiable install/upgrade flow for Redwood Private. Redwood outlined the proposed offline bundle format (bundle + mani...
text: Helios Defense walked through their air-gapped environment constraints (no outbound network, controlled inbound media, strict change control, limited time sync) and asked for an end-to-end, offline-verifiable install/upgrade flow for Redwood Private. Redwood outlined the proposed offline bundle format (bundle + manifest + detached signature), trust-root handling, and installer preflight verification that fails closed on mismatch. Helios clarified acceptance criteria: deterministic artifact list, offline integrity verification steps they can run independently, clear audit evidence on install, and an approved break-glass path with logging. Open questions centered on key rotation in air-gapped sites, handling time skew for signature validity, and how downgrade/replay protection will work with a local install state.

title: Northbridge Capital Markets - Private deployment security review (model change audit + release notes)
preview: Northbridge reviewed Redwood Private change-management controls focused on: (1) audit logging for model metadata updates and version promotions, (2) retention and SIEM export options, and (3) how customer-visible release notes are generated without leaking internal details. Redwood explained the v1 model metadata sc...
text: Northbridge reviewed Redwood Private change-management controls focused on: (1) audit logging for model metadata updates and version promotions, (2) retention and SIEM export options, and (3) how customer-visible release notes are generated without leaking internal details. Redwood explained the v1 model metadata schema and event-driven changelog generator, including public vs internal fields, RBAC, and idempotent eventing. Northbridge asked for: exact event fields (actor, approval, diff), retention guarantees in Private, offline/air-gapped export, and assurance that pinned versions cannot be changed silently. Next steps: Redwood to share an example audit event + sample release note artifact, confirm Private retention configuration and SIEM forwarding patterns, and provide a short write-up mapping release notes to upgrade bundles/rollback documentation.

title: Velox Systems - credits to first-call & hosting strategy
preview: Intro discovery covering Redwood quickstart credits, time-to-first-token (TTFT), and tradeoffs between Hosted, Dedicated, and Private/VPC. Security concerns (KMS, audit logs, SSO) and expected throughput discussed. Agreed next step: share pricing and run a small benchmark with sample payloads.
text: Intro discovery covering Redwood quickstart credits, time-to-first-token (TTFT), and tradeoffs between Hosted, Dedicated, and Private/VPC. title: Northstar Fintech - Procurement + SLA language (Dedicated bounds: min baseline / max ceiling)
preview: Northstar and Redwood aligned on contract language for Dedicated autoscaling bounds: customer-configurable min baseline and hard max ceiling (spend control) enforced by the autoscaler, plus optional predictive scaling that cannot violate bounds. Legal/procurement asked for (1) explicit statement Redwood will not sca...
text: Northstar and Redwood aligned on contract language for Dedicated autoscaling bounds: customer-configurable min baseline and hard max ceiling (spend control) enforced by the autoscaler, plus optional predictive scaling that cannot violate bounds. Legal/procurement asked for (1) explicit statement Redwood will not scale above max without written customer approval, (2) behavior/notifications when minimum cannot be met due to regional GPU shortage, (3) SLA measurement and service credits, and (4) auditability/RBAC around scaling policy changes. Redwood proposed wording that max is a hard invariant; min is a target with a defined degraded-state if capacity shortage occurs, with alerts + operational escalation and optional burst options. Next steps: Redwood to send redlines (SLA + Order Form exhibit) and a one-pager describing bounds semantics and audit log events; Northstar to confirm pref...

title: Lumen Legaltech - Security review: audit logs + config change controls (Dedicated autoscaling)
preview: Lumen Legaltech reviewed Redwood’s approach to auditability for Dedicated autoscaling configuration and scaling decisions, with emphasis on compliance needs (change traceability, retention, export). Redwood confirmed that scaling policy updates are captured as audit events with actor identity (user/service), before/...
text: Lumen Legaltech reviewed Redwood’s approach to auditability for Dedicated autoscaling configuration and scaling decisions, with emphasis on compliance needs (change traceability, retention, export). Redwood confirmed that scaling policy updates are captured as audit events with actor identity (user/service), before/after diffs, request IDs, and enforcement outcomes. Redwood also described decision/audit events emitted by the autoscaler (requested desired vs bounded desired, clamp reason, predictive vs reactive signal source, staleness/fallback). Lumen asked about RBAC granularity, separation of duties, and whether Redwood support staff can modify customer bounds. Redwood outlined role-based controls in Console/API, optional approval workflows via customer process, and that Redwood internal access is controlled and logged. Next steps: share draft audit event schema and retention option...

title: Security review: logging + retention (Redwood Private)
preview: NorthBridge Financial reviewed Redwood Private logging behavior, retention defaults, and the split between audit logs and operational logs. Customer requirements emphasized: no prompt/response logging by default, deterministic redaction, export to Splunk HEC in VPC and in an on-prem/air-gapped environment via a forw...
text: NorthBridge Financial reviewed Redwood Private logging behavior, retention defaults, and the split between audit logs and operational logs. Customer requirements emphasized: no prompt/response logging by default, deterministic redaction, export to Splunk HEC in VPC and in an on-prem/air-gapped environment via a forward proxy, and customer-owned retention controls. Redwood clarified: structured operational logs are emitted from control plane + data plane; payload logging is disabled by default; tenant identifiers are hashed; audit logs are separate from operational logs and intended for security-relevant events (auth, config changes, admin actions). Export options discussed included Splunk HEC and Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, with OTLP as an optional path depending on customer tooling. Next steps included sharing a field-level log schema and a retention/redaction guidance doc, plus confir...

Redwood reviewed planned upgrade lifecycle events (upgrade started, prechecks pass/fail, backup started/completed, migration start/end, rollback invoked/completed), and discussed required fields to make logs useful in Splunk (who, what version, where, correlation IDs). title: Cobalt Finance — Security review: Dedicated Burst audit logging + RBAC
preview: Cobalt Finance security team reviewed Redwood’s audit logging and RBAC model for the Dedicated “Burst Capacity” feature. Customer asked for: (1) concrete audit event types for burst config changes and admin overrides, (2) who can make changes (RBAC + SSO/SAML), (3) retention options and export to their SIEM, and (4)...
text: Cobalt Finance security team reviewed Redwood’s audit logging and RBAC model for the Dedicated “Burst Capacity” feature. Customer asked for: (1) concrete audit event types for burst config changes and admin overrides, (2) who can make changes (RBAC + SSO/SAML), (3) retention options and export to their SIEM, and (4) evidence that customer admins can independently audit changes vs Redwood Support actions. Redwood explained planned audit events (enable/disable, limit updates, cooldown/ratio changes, tier changes, policy override, emergency circuit breaker), separation of duties (customer admin vs Redwood operator), read vs write permissions, and log export/retention approach (Console + API export, with longer retention for enterprise). Next steps include sharing a sample audit event payload/redacted examples and confirming Cobalt’s retention and SIEM ingestion requirements.

title: Security review - audit logging for route experiments (Helio Finance)
preview: Helio Finance ran a security/compliance review focused on audit logging and change management for Redwood Console route-level experiments (A/B testing at routing layer). Helio asked what experiment lifecycle/config change events are logged, which fields are included (actor identity, source IP, request IDs, before/af...
text: Helio Finance ran a security/compliance review focused on audit logging and change management for Redwood Console route-level experiments (A/B testing at routing layer). Helio asked what experiment lifecycle/config change events are logged, which fields are included (actor identity, source IP, request IDs, before/after diff), retention periods, and how audit events can be exported to their SIEM. Redwood confirmed audit events exist for create/update/start/stop/delete and RBAC denials, support SSO/SAML-based actor identity, and export via audit log export pipeline (scheduled export + API). Open items: provide sample audit event payloads (redacted), retention options for enterprise, and documentation evidence (SOC 2, audit logging spec, and data flow).

title: Security review: Quantization Profiles change controls + audit logs (FalconBank)
preview: FalconBank security review focused on change control for the opt-in Quantization Profile Library. Customer asked how Redwood enforces RBAC for route-level enablement, what gets written to audit logs (fields, retention, export), whether quantization can be globally disabled, and how Redwood prevents silent profile ch...
text: FalconBank security review focused on change control for the opt-in Quantization Profile Library. Customer asked how Redwood enforces RBAC for route-level enablement, what gets written to audit logs (fields, retention, export), whether quantization can be globally disabled, and how Redwood prevents silent profile changes. Redwood confirmed: (1) route-level toggles only (no default-on), (2) deterministic resolution with compatibility guardrails and safe fallback to baseline, (3) immutable, versioned profile IDs (with signing/integrity checks) and no silent upgrades when pinning a specific profile, (4) audit events with actor, route, before/after diff, and outcome (applied/fallback/disabled), (5) global kill-switch + per-tenant override option, and (6) Console change history view and API-driven configuration for internal approvals.

text: Emberfin (regulated fintech) reviewed Redwood’s audit logging and governance model for Hosted canary rollouts (percentage traffic splitting). text: Enterprise security review with Ironbank Financial covering Redwood’s RBAC v2 direction (least privilege, scoped role bindings, deny-by-default), SSO/SAML integration and group-to-role mapping expectations, and audit logging requirements for evidence and SIEM ingestion. Ironbank requested concrete examples: permission catalog granularity, audit event schema including authorization decision context, and operational controls like break-glass access + review. Redwood clarified hosted vs dedicated vs private semantics and current roadmap/timing for RBAC v2 rollout and migration compatibility.

text: Lattice Search walked Redwood through their internal go-live/security approval requirements for adding an LLM inference provider to an enterprise search product (customer data, regulated customers). Key asks: auditability (who did what / when), SSO/SAML + RBAC, data retention and residency clarity, and a concrete “launch checklist” they can attach to their change-management packet. Redwood clarified Hosted API data handling (no prompt storage by default; limited logs), available security controls today (SOC 2, encryption, key mgmt options depending on deployment), and what’s on the near-term roadmap (richer audit logs in Console, admin controls). Discussion also covered production readiness items: retries/timeouts, rate limiting/quotas, streaming disconnect behavior, and model pinning/fallback. Agreed to share Redwood’s security artifacts + draft production launch checklist, and sched...

title: Photon Health x Redwood — Private deployment compliance deep dive (retention, audit logs, report pack)
preview: Photon Health drilled into Redwood Private deployment specifics: where different log/telemetry data classes are stored in Private, how retention policies are evaluated/enforced (control plane policy + data plane purge jobs), how deletions are verified, and how the baseline compliance reporting pack will be produced ...
text: Photon Health drilled into Redwood Private deployment specifics: where different log/telemetry data classes are stored in Private, how retention policies are evaluated/enforced (control plane policy + data plane purge jobs), how deletions are verified, and how the baseline compliance reporting pack will be produced and delivered for customer audits. Photon emphasized requirements around PHI-adjacent prompt data, audit log completeness, evidence repeatability, and a preference for customer-managed storage/KMS with tight RBAC. Redwood clarified that in Private, data plane stores live in Photon’s account/VPC, Redwood provides the policy service + purge job framework, and the compliance pack is generated from audit log exporter outputs plus retention configuration exports, with versioned templates.

text: Northbridge Bank walked through their security review questions focused on data retention, audit logging, and evidence expectations for regulated workloads. Redwood clarified which data classes are in scope for retention controls (request/response logs where enabled, audit logs, traces/metrics, and certain artifacts), how default TTLs differ by Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private, and how purge jobs are enforced and verified. Northbridge emphasized need for (a) customer-configurable retention with minimum/maximum guardrails, (b) a clear legal hold / incident hold process, (c) audit-log export coverage including SSO/RBAC/admin actions, and (d) a customer-ready evidence package aligned to SOC 2 / ISO-style controls. text: Nordholm Payments reviewed Redwood Hosted controls for EU-only processing, audit logs, and failover behavior. Redwood confirmed planned/active region pinning controls (primary region + allowed region set) with deterministic enforcement at request time and blocked cross-region fallback by default. Customer requested documentation: residency policy/controls narrative, audit log event schema + samples, subprocessor region list, SOC 2 report, and retention details for logs/traces. Next steps: Redwood to send residency FAQ + internal policy excerpt, schedule a short technical follow-up on audit log export + SIEM integration, and confirm timeline for GA of hosted residency enforcement.
